scope_note: QA Egyptian Ennead Orbit Partition cert [314]. Primary sources: Iverson, B. (1975-1996) Quantum Arithmetic vols 1-2 (QA-1, QA-2); Budge, E.A.W. (1904) The Gods of the Egyptians, Methuen, Vol. 1, pp.86-105; Wilkinson, R.H. (2003) The Complete Gods and Goddesses of Ancient Egypt, Thames & Hudson, ISBN 978-0-500-05120-7, pp.74-81. QA mapping: The Ennead of Heliopolis (9 primary Egyptian deities) is partitioned as 1 source-god (Atum, self-created) + 8 active gods. The QA mod-9 orbit partition over {1,...,9}^2 (81 states total) splits identically: Singularity = {(9,9)} (1 T-fixed state, T-period=1) + Satellite = {3,6,9}^2 \ {(9,9)} (8 states, T-period=8) + Cosmos (72 states, T-period=24). T-step A1-compliant: (b,e) -> (e, ((b+e-1)%9)+1). Orbit family by 3-divisibility: Singularity iff 9|b and 9|e; Satellite iff 3|b and 3|e and not Singularity; Cosmos otherwise. CLAIM: (C1) |Satellite|+|Singularity|=8+1=9 (Ennead cardinality); total partition=81. (C2) (9,9) is the unique T-fixed point in {1,...,9}^2; no other state satisfies T(b,e)=(b,e). (C3) Orbit from (3,3) has period 8 and visits all 8 Satellite states (3,3)->(3,6)->(6,9)->(9,6)->(6,6)->(6,3)->(3,9)->(9,3)->(3,3). (C4) (b,e) in Satellite iff 3|b and 3|e and (b,e)!=(9,9); partition counts {1,8,72} verified exhaustively. (C5) Theorem NT: deity attributes (domain, iconography) are observer projections; orbit_family(b,e) and T-period are the discrete QA claims; no float state in QA layer. Lineage: derived 2026-06-04 from Iverson QA-1/QA-2 orbit structure + Budge/Wilkinson Ennead primary sources. Builds on cert [298] (orbit grade decomposition, 1+8+72 v3-stratification).
